scenario: Nimbus Marketplace models a Camunda 8.8 DMN table that picks 1 of 6 dish recommendations per order across 7200 orders per day. A modeler wants the column header in the modeler UI to read Dish while the variable name stays desiredDish, and the team must pick the right attribute on the output element to hold that short human readable description.
question: Which attribute on the output element holds the short human readable description shown as the column header in a Camunda 8.8 DMN decision table?

options.text: The name attribute carries the short description used as the column header while the label attribute stores the variable reference for downstream processes
The id attribute carries the short description used as the column header while the typeRef attribute stores the variable reference for downstream processes
The typeRef attribute carries the short description used as the column header while the name attribute stores the variable reference for downstream processes
The label attribute carries the short description used as the column header while the name attribute stores the variable reference for downstream processes

a.text: Incorrect. The name attribute stores the variable identifier used to reference the output value; the label is what the modeler sees as the column header.

b.text: Incorrect. The id attribute is a technical XML identifier and typeRef holds a data type; neither carries the short human readable description on the output.

c.text: Incorrect. The typeRef attribute declares a data type, not a description, and the variable reference lives in the name attribute on the output element.

d.text: Correct. The docs describe the label attribute on the output element as a short description, recommended but not required, while name carries the variable reference.

explanation: The Camunda 8.8 DMN docs reserve the label attribute on the output element for a short human readable description shown in the modeler, while the name attribute carries the variable reference used by downstream processes.
